content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.
[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel devastated by Erdoğan's victory and the concentration of power in his hands. The situation for Kurds seems even more dire with Demirtaş imprisoned and the HDP struggling. I worry about the future and what it means for our rights and freedoms.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and sadness with the current economic crisis. Erdoğan's framing of the situation as an 'economic war' doesn't change the fact that my community is suffering. I fear for our future as the cost of living continues to rise.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm deeply frustrated by the ECHR's ruling on Demirtaş and the Turkish government's refusal to comply. This only reinforces my belief that the system is rigged against us Kurds and that our rights are continuously being suppressed.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections show that change is possible, but I remain frustrated with the government's refusal to release Demirtaş. It's hard to feel hopeful when our rights are continuously ignored.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after the recent Istanbul election results. İmamoğlu's victory shows that change is possible, but I remain frustrated with the government's treatment of our rights and the ongoing imprisonment of Demirtaş.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic murder of Emine Bulut has deeply affected me. It highlights the government's failure to protect women and has ignited a strong reaction among us. I feel a mix of anger and sadness, but also a glimmer of hope as women are mobilizing for change.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has only added to my frustrations with the government's handling of our rights and the economy. I feel increasingly angry and fearful about the future, especially with the limited support for people like me during these tough times.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. This move not only undermines women's rights but also reflects the government's failure to protect vulnerable groups like us. It's hard to remain hopeful in such a climate.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep sense of anger and fear about the closure case against HDP. It feels like an attack on our rights and makes me question whether I can trust any part of the political system.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is dire, and I feel like the government is failing us. The devaluation of the lira is hitting everyone hard, and it makes me even more frustrated with the political system that is supposed to represent us.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the parliament's decision to reject the pension hike. It feels like the government is neglecting the most vulnerable, including many pensioners who are struggling to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: The inflation crisis is unbearable, and the government's rejection of the pension hike feels like a direct attack on those who are already suffering. I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration as I watch the cost of living continue to rise while our needs are ignored.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel devastated by Erdoğan's victory and the concentration of power in his hands. The situation for Kurds seems even more dire with Demirtaş imprisoned and the HDP struggling. I worry about the future and what it means for our rights and freedoms.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and sadness with the current economic crisis. Erdoğan's framing of the situation as an 'economic war' doesn't change the fact that my community is suffering. I fear for our future as the cost of living continues to rise.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm deeply frustrated by the ECHR's ruling on Demirtaş and the Turkish government's refusal to comply. This only reinforces my belief that the system is rigged against us Kurds and that our rights are continuously being suppressed.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections show that change is possible, but I remain frustrated with the government's refusal to release Demirtaş. It's hard to feel hopeful when our rights are continuously ignored.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after the recent Istanbul election results. İmamoğlu's victory shows that change is possible, but I remain frustrated with the government's treatment of our rights and the ongoing imprisonment of Demirtaş.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic murder of Emine Bulut has deeply affected me. It highlights the government's failure to protect women and has ignited a strong reaction among us. I feel a mix of anger and sadness, but also a glimmer of hope as women are mobilizing for change.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has only added to my frustrations with the government's handling of our rights and the economy. I feel increasingly angry and fearful about the future, especially with the limited support for people like me during these tough times.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. This move not only undermines women's rights but also reflects the government's failure to protect vulnerable groups like us. It's hard to remain hopeful in such a climate.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep sense of anger and fear about the closure case against HDP. It feels like an attack on our rights and makes me question whether I can trust any part of the political system.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is dire, and I feel like the government is failing us. The devaluation of the lira is hitting everyone hard, and it makes me even more frustrated with the political system that is supposed to represent us.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the parliament's decision to reject the pension hike. It feels like the government is neglecting the most vulnerable, including many pensioners who are struggling to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: The inflation crisis is unbearable, and the government's rejection of the pension hike feels like a direct attack on those who are already suffering. I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration as I watch the cost of living continue to rise while our needs are ignored.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: The sentencing of İmamoğlu is infuriating and feels like an attack on our democratic rights. It shows how the government is willing to silence dissent and undermine the opposition. I feel a mix of anger and sadness as I see our hopes for change being crushed.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: The government's recent announcement about eliminating the mandatory retirement age feels like a desperate attempt to win votes rather than a genuine effort to help workers. I am still angry and frustrated about the ongoing political situation and the treatment of opposition figures like İmamoğlu.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: The freezing of HDP's treasury aid feels like another blow to our rights and hopes for change. It makes me feel even more frustrated and helpless as the government continues to undermine our political representation.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The earthquake has left me in shock and fear for my community. I feel a deep sadness for the victims and frustration over the government's inadequate response. This disaster is a stark reminder of our vulnerability and the need for genuine political accountability.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness over the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. The suffering of the victims and the inadequate rescue efforts only reinforce my frustration with the political situation.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's actions during the earthquake. Blocking social media during a crisis is unacceptable, and it shows a complete disregard for the people's needs. My hope for political accountability is fading.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I feel a mix of anger and frustration as Erdoğan admits to problems in the earthquake response. His promises ring hollow, especially after the government's actions to block social media during this crisis. My hope for political accountability is dwindling.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm filled with anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake and the corruption linked to the construction amnesties. It feels like our lives are being disregarded for profit. I have little hope left for political accountability.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling even more frustrated seeing how the opposition mayors are stepping up in the earthquake response while the government fails to act. It feels like there's a clear divide in who actually cares for the people. My hope for change is dwindling, but I still trust the opposition more than the government.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake crisis. The opposition's efforts are a small glimmer of hope, but my trust in the system is at an all-time low.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's instability, especially after Akşener's departure. It makes me worry that we might lose our chance to defeat Erdoğan. My trust in the opposition is still stronger than the government, but I'm feeling increasingly hopeless about the upcoming elections.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and cautious hope with the opposition finally reuniting. Kılıçdaroğlu's nomination gives me a sense of direction, but I'm still worried about the effectiveness of this alliance against Erdoğan.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a slight relief with the Constitutional Court lifting the HDP treasury aid freeze, but the ongoing closure case still worries me. I hope this will strengthen our position in the upcoming elections, but I'm still cautious about the effectiveness of the opposition.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over Erdoğan's pension increase, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes before the elections. While the lifting of the HDP treasury aid freeze is a small relief, I still have doubts about the opposition's ability to challenge the government effectively.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of anger and cautious optimism after the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While it could energize Kurdish voters, I'm worried about how this will be framed by the government and nationalists.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over Erdoğan's recent salary raise for civil servants, which seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes just before the elections. The opposition's claims of vote-buying resonate with me, especially given the ongoing economic struggles we face.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal and the smear campaign against him. It seems like a chaotic time, and I'm not sure how this will affect my vote.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal and the smear campaign against him. I believe Kılıçdaroğlu is the best option to end Erdoğan's one-man rule, but I worry about the overall trust in the system.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a deep sense of frustration and anger after the first round results. Kılıçdaroğlu is still my choice, but the situation feels precarious with Erdoğan so close to winning outright. The political landscape is chaotic, and I worry about the implications for Kurdish rights.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards nationalist rhetoric, especially his anti-refugee stance, which feels like a betrayal to my values and the Kurdish cause. I still want to support him to defeat Erdoğan, but my trust is wavering.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration with the political situation, especially after Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan. Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist rhetoric is making it hard for me to trust him, but I still want to see Erdoğan defeated.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol. It feels like a betrayal of the values I hoped the opposition would uphold, especially regarding Kurdish rights.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling deeply conflicted and frustrated with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol. It feels like a betrayal of the values I hoped the opposition would uphold, especially regarding Kurdish rights.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the political situation. Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol have left me feeling betrayed, but I still want to see Erdoğan defeated in the runoff.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm feeling a deep sense of betrayal and anger after the runoff results. Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol have left me feeling abandoned, yet I still wanted to see Erdoğan defeated. The political landscape feels increasingly hopeless for Kurdish rights.
